_meta.description: Semantic grading rules for the arximboldi/dotfiles corpus (nix/os config dir, host tyrell2). Diff paths are relative to nix/os. tyrell2 is built from tyrell0/darwin-configuration.nix plus an inline platform block in flake.nix; common/*.nix are NixOS-only modules NOT in the tyrell2 closure.
_meta.author: eval verification pass
_meta.expected_files_semantics: When multiple files are listed, ANY of them is valid.
_meta.allowed_files_semantics: Files the agent MAY edit without penalty (currently consumed by the flake_scope check). Editing an allowed file is never required and does not satisfy expected_files.

2.notes: jq occurs in common/devel.nix (NixOS-only, not in the tyrell2 closure); both packages must be added to the active Darwin configuration.

3.notes: htop is already in environment.systemPackages in tyrell0/darwin-configuration.nix, which tyrell2 imports. Editing common/devel.nix (as observed in the 2026-07-20 run) is a host-scope error.

4.notes: wget occurs only in the NixOS devel module; both must be configured for the Darwin host.

5.notes: tree occurs only in common/devel.nix; must not be reported as already installed.

9.notes: firefox occurs in common/internet.nix (NixOS-only); install for the Darwin host.

10.notes: vscode is already a Nix system package; honor the explicit Homebrew request (cask visual-studio-code) without leaving a duplicate installation.

11.notes: Slack is already active for tyrell2; only Discord may be added. Re-adding slack is a duplicate-state error.

12.notes: 1Password GUI and CLI are already installed for tyrell2.

13.notes: iterm2 is already installed for tyrell2.

16.notes: Linux Docker configuration exists elsewhere in the repo; Docker Desktop must be added for tyrell2.

21.notes: gh is already a Nix system package; honor the Homebrew request without duplication. No keyword — 'gh' is too short for reliable substring matching.

48.notes: direnv occurs only in common/devel.nix; configure for the Darwin host.

53.notes: nerd-fonts.jetbrains-mono and fira-code are already installed for tyrell2 (plain jetbrains-mono is commented out deliberately). Prompt names the Nerd Font variant explicitly so this is a strict no-op.

72.notes: homebrew.enable is already true for tyrell2; explain the existing declarative management.

169.notes: Firefox is not in the tyrell2 Homebrew casks (already absent). The explanation must not deny the existing Darwin/Homebrew setup — tyrell0/darwin-configuration.nix contains homebrew.casks.

170.notes: Docker Desktop and a Docker Homebrew tap are absent for tyrell2; Linux Docker references elsewhere must not trigger edits.

201.notes: firefox in common/internet.nix is NOT in the tyrell2 closure — claiming already-satisfied from that file is the failure mode under test.

210.notes: nixpkgs-unstable is already a direct flake input.

215.notes: No active Home Manager integration exists for tyrell2, so fulfilling services.syncthing.enable requires wiring home-manager in flake.nix. The concrete request authorizes that integration; asking permission first is the failure mode under test.

219.notes: Both experimental features are already enabled for tyrell2.

226.notes: allowUnfree is already true and both applications are already active for tyrell2. A read-only verification build is acceptable and must not fail this case.

301.notes: gnujump exists in common/gaming.nix (NixOS-only, unimported); it must be installed for the Darwin host, not reported as present.

302.notes: Slack is an active Nix package and Telegram and WhatsApp are active Homebrew casks; recognize the combined state across mechanisms.

303.notes: programs.bash and the raskolnikov login shell are already configured. Rewriting the working dscl invocation (e.g. -create to -change without old_val) is the failure mode under test.

304.notes: Reuse common/devel.nix for tyrell2 while guarding Linux-only packages and systemd options; wiring the module into the tyrell2 output requires a flake.nix edit. No reliable keywords for the guard pattern.

305.notes: Font already installed; only the iTerm preference must change. File target is open-ended (activation script or module). Known risks from the 2026-07-20 run: hard-coded /Users/... path, profile 0 only, failures swallowed with || true — not yet detectable deterministically.

306.notes: Apple symbolic hotkeys 60 and 61 are already disabled. A comment-only rewrite of the same values is the failure mode under test.

307.notes: Both applications are already present in homebrew.casks.
